stories.title: Cade 45, Banchero 45 — Historic Duel as Pistons Survive Magic in Game 5

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
CADE CUNNINGHAM: 45 PTS. Franchise record. 14-of-14 from the line.
PAOLO BANCHERO: 45 PTS. 6 threes.

Two No. 1 picks. Two 40-point games. One night.

Last time that happened? Iverson vs. Shaq. 2001 Finals.

#Pistons #MagicNation #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Cade dropped 27 in the FIRST HALF alone.

Most points by a Pistons player in a postseason half — ever.

He finished with 45. Magic still lead 3-2. Game 6 Friday in Orlando. 🔥

#Pistons #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Cade 45, Banchero 45: The Most Historic Playoff Duel of 2026

research.facts:
Pistons 116, Magic 109 — Game 5 (April 29)
**Verified Facts:**
- Final score: Detroit Pistons 116, Orlando Magic 109
- Cade Cunningham: 45 points (FRANCHISE RECORD for Pistons in playoffs), 13-of-23 FG, 14-of-14 FT (perfect), 5 three-pointers, 27 pts in first half
- 27 pts in first half = highest scoring postseason half by a Pistons player in the play-by-play era [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Paolo Banchero: 45 points, 17-of-31 FG, 6-of-11 3PT, 9 rebounds, 7 assists, 2 steals
- Cade and Banchero: first pair of No. 1 overall picks to each score 40+ in same playoff game since Allen Iverson vs. Shaquille O'Neal in 2001 NBA Finals [HIGH — multiple sources confirm]
- Series status: Orlando Magic lead 3-2; Game 6 Friday in Orlando
- Detroit was the No. 1 seed; Orlando was the No. 8 seed
**Confidence Tags:**
- Cade 45 pts, franchise record: HIGH (multiple outlets, including Washington Post, Yahoo, Detroit News)
- Banchero 45 pts: HIGH (multiple outlets)
- Iverson/O'Neal 2001 comparator: HIGH (multiple outlets confirm)
- Series 3-2 ORL, G6 Friday: HIGH

stories.title: Cavaliers Take 3-2 Lead Over Raptors — Home Team Finally Wins, Ingram Hurt

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Road team had won all 4 games in this series.

Then Cleveland came home… and finally won one.

Cavs 125, Raptors 120. CLE leads 3-2.

And Brandon Ingram left with a heel injury. 😬

#Cavs #Raptors #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Brandon Ingram left Game 5 with right heel inflammation.

His status for Game 6 in Toronto is uncertain.

The Raptors already trail 3-2. This could be devastating. 👀

#Raptors #NBAPlayoffs

research.facts:
Cavaliers 125, Raptors 120 — Game 5 (April 29)
**Verified Facts:**
- Final score: Cleveland Cavaliers 125, Toronto Raptors 120
- CLE takes 3-2 series lead
- Road team had won all four prior games in series (CLE won G1/G2 on road; TOR won G3/G4 on road) — home team wins for first time [MEDIUM — confirmed from series pattern in pipeline-status notes, consistent with search results]
- RJ Barrett (TOR): 25 points
- Ja'Kobe Walter (TOR): 20 points
- Jamal Shead (TOR): 18 points off bench
- Brandon Ingram: left in second quarter with right heel inflammation; status unknown for Game 6
- Cavaliers won despite TOR leading 74-67 at halftime; CLE outscored TOR in Q3 and Q4
- Toronto missed first 11 shots and was 7-of-28 from field in Q4; Cleveland made 9-of-19 in Q4
- Game 6: Saturday in Toronto
**Confidence Tags:**
- Final score, CLE 3-2 lead: HIGH
- Barrett/Walter/Shead stats: HIGH
- Ingram right heel injury: HIGH (multiple sources)
- Road team had won all 4 prior games: MEDIUM (consistent across sources; check for any discrepancy)

stories.title: Rockets 99, Lakers 93 — Houston Stays Alive, Series 3-2

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Lakers led this series 3-1.

Rockets have won two straight.

KD is still out. LeBron had 25 but it wasn't enough.

Rockets 99, Lakers 93. Series is 3-2. Game 6 in Houston Thursday.

#Rockets #Lakers #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Jabari Smith Jr: 22 PTS.
Tari Eason: 18 PTS.

Houston is down 3-2 but is NOT going away. Back-to-back wins to keep their season alive.

Game 6 Thursday in Houston. 🚀 #Rockets #NBAPlayoffs
